data.title: Budget Tracker
data.statement:
A student is trying to save up money for a new game over 7 days. Calculate their total weekly allowance, total expenses, and overall savings.

<<Note:>> This problem uses basic arithmetic, just sum the values and find the difference.

testCases.title: Standard Values
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 4250
Total Expenses: 2850
Weekly Savings: 1400

testCases.title: Positive Weekly Savings
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 3820
Total Expenses: 2260
Weekly Savings: 1560

testCases.title: Zero Values
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 900
Total Expenses: 450
Weekly Savings: 450

testCases.title: Zero Savings
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 3000
Total Expenses: 3000
Weekly Savings: 0

testCases.title: All Zeros
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 0
Total Expenses: 0
Weekly Savings: 0

Total Allowance: 0
Total Expenses: 0
Weekly Savings: 0

testCases.title: Negative Savings
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 910
Total Expenses: 1110
Weekly Savings: -200

testCases.title: Overspent
testCases.testOut:
Total Allowance: 1450
Total Expenses: 2050
Weekly Savings: -600

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

A student is trying to save up money for a new game over 7 days. Calculate their total weekly allowance, total expenses, and overall savings.

Note: This problem uses basic arithmetic, just sum the values and find the difference.
Input
Line 1: Each line contains an integer allow, the daily allowance, listed in order from Monday to Sunday.
Line 2: Each line contains an integer exp, the daily expenses, listed in order from Monday to Sunday.
Output
Total Allowance: followed by the total of the daily allowances.
Total Expenses: followed by the total of the daily expenses.
Total Savings: followed by the total of the Weekly savings.
Constraints
0 ≤ allow ≤ 1000
0 ≤ exp ≤ 1000
Example
Input
500
600
450
550
700
800
650
300
400
250
350
500
600
450
Output
Total Allowance: 4250
Total Expenses: 2850
Weekly Savings: 1400
